items.text: GIVE BLOOD, GET A SHIRT! Campus Blood Drive. May 15-16, 10 AM-4 PM. Anderson Hall Lobby. All donors receive a free T-shirt and a $5 campus dining voucher. Walk-ins welcome. Bring ID. Sponsored by the Student Health Office.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what do donors receive?

options.A: A free meal at a campus restaurant
options.B: A parking pass for Anderson Hall
options.C: A health screening from the Student Health Office
options.D: A dining voucher and a complimentary shirt

questions.explanation: The flyer states donors receive 'a free T-shirt and a $5 campus dining voucher.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the blood drive?

options.B: It is only for students with valid health insurance.
options.C: It takes place over a single afternoon.
options.D: The dining voucher must be used on May 15.

questions.explanation: The flyer says 'Walk-ins welcome,' which implies people can participate without a prior appointment.

items.text: STUDENT THEATER PRESENTS: 'Midnight Library'! Shows: March 3-7 at 7:30 PM. University Theater, Room 204. Tickets: $5 for students, $10 general. Purchase at the door or online. Seating is limited. Directed by Leo Chen.

questions.stem: Where will the theater performances be held?

options.B: At an off-campus venue
options.C: In Anderson Hall Lobby

questions.explanation: The flyer specifies the location as 'University Theater, Room 204.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about ticket availability?

options.A: It might be wise to buy tickets in advance.
options.B: Online tickets are more expensive than door tickets.
options.C: Only students are permitted to attend.
options.D: Tickets are free for university staff.

questions.explanation: The notice that 'Seating is limited' suggests tickets could sell out, making advance purchase a good idea.

items.text: Dear Biology 101 Students, This is a reminder for the mandatory lab safety training. It will be held this Friday at 2:00 PM in Science Building, Room 105. Please bring your student ID. Attendance will be recorded. Regards, Dr. Park

questions.stem: What are students instructed to bring to the training?

options.B: Their student identification card
options.C: Personal protective equipment

questions.explanation: The email explicitly states, 'Please bring your student ID.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the training session?

options.A: It is optional for most students.
options.B: It will be led by a teaching assistant.
options.C: Student presence will be officially noted.
options.D: It will last for several hours.

questions.explanation: The email says 'Attendance will be recorded,' which means their presence will be officially noted.

items.text: Hi Sam, Thank you for volunteering as a campus tour guide. Your shift is confirmed for Saturday, April 10, from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM. Meet at the Welcome Center. You'll receive a $10 lunch stipend. Please confirm. Best, Ms. Chen

questions.stem: What time does Sam's volunteer shift begin?

options.B: At 9:00 in the morning
options.C: At 10:00 in the morning

questions.explanation: The email states the shift is 'from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM.'

questions.stem: What is Sam most likely expected to do after reading this email?

options.A: Purchase a uniform for the tour.
options.B: Reply to confirm the shift details.
options.C: Go directly to the Welcome Center on Saturday.
options.D: Collect the lunch stipend in advance.

questions.explanation: The email ends with the instruction 'Please confirm,' implying Sam should reply.

items.text: NOTICE: The South Lot B parking area will be closed for repaving from May 15 to May 17. Alternative parking is available in the North Garage. All vehicles must be removed by 7:00 PM on May 14.

questions.stem: Where should drivers park during the closure?

options.A: In the adjacent South Lot A
options.B: At the Anderson Hall visitor lot
options.C: In the North Garage

questions.explanation: The notice directly states 'Alternative parking is available in the North Garage.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the notice?

options.A: The work will be completed early.
options.B: The lot will be inaccessible for multiple days.
options.C: Only faculty cars need to be moved.
options.D: The repaving was scheduled by students.

questions.explanation: The closure dates 'from May 15 to May 17' indicate a multi-day period where the lot cannot be used.

items.text: LIBRARY HOURS ADJUSTMENT: During Spring Break, March 11-15, the main library will operate from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily. Regular hours resume on Monday, March 18. The 24-hour study room in Anderson Hall will remain accessible.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when do regular library hours resume?

options.B: At the end of Spring Break
options.C: After the 24-hour room closes

questions.explanation: The notice clearly states 'Regular hours resume on Monday, March 18.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the library's Spring Break schedule?

options.A: It is more convenient for most students.
options.B: The building is open for fewer hours.
options.C: All study spaces will be closed.

questions.explanation: The notice specifies reduced hours ('10:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily') compared to typical, longer library hours.

items.text: NETWORK MAINTENANCE: Campus Wi-Fi will be unavailable in all academic buildings from 11:00 PM on Saturday, May 4, until 5:00 AM on Sunday, May 5, for essential system upgrades. Please plan your work accordingly. We apologize for the inconvenience.

questions.stem: When will the Wi-Fi service interruption begin?

options.B: Early on Sunday morning
options.C: Throughout the weekend

questions.explanation: The notice specifies the Wi-Fi will be unavailable 'from 11:00 PM on Saturday, May 4.'

questions.stem: The word 'essential' in the notice is closest in meaning to

options.B: complicated
options.C: optional

questions.explanation: In this context, 'essential' describes upgrades that are crucial or required, making 'necessary' the best synonym.

items.text: Due to road construction, the Green Line campus shuttle will be rerouted from May 15-19. Stops at Anderson Hall and South Lot B are temporarily suspended. A temporary stop will be established at the Union Building. A $5 late fee applies for parking pass adjustments not made by Friday.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is a consequence of the shuttle detour?

options.A: To receive a refund for the shuttle pass
options.B: To use a temporary stop at the Union Building
options.C: To avoid a parking ticket at Anderson Hall
options.D: To pay a higher fare for the Green Line

questions.explanation: The notice states a temporary stop will be established at the Union Building due to the rerouting.

questions.stem: What specific action must be taken by Friday to avoid a penalty?

options.A: To adjust parking passes for a fee waiver
options.B: To register for an alternative shuttle route
options.C: To submit a construction complaint form
options.D: To confirm attendance at a facilities meeting

questions.explanation: The notice indicates a $5 late fee applies for parking pass adjustments not made by Friday.

items.text: Dear Student, Your meal plan has a remaining balance of $42.75 for this semester. To receive a refund, please complete the online form by March 10. Refunds will be processed within 10 business days. Sincerely, Ms. Chen, Dining Services

questions.stem: What is the stated purpose of the online form?

options.A: To request a refund for a meal plan balance
options.B: To renew the meal plan for the next semester
options.C: To report an issue with dining service quality
options.D: To check the current balance on the card

questions.explanation: The email says to complete the online form to receive a refund for the remaining balance.

questions.stem: According to the email, by when must the form be submitted?

options.B: Before the end of the current semester
options.C: By the specified date of March 10
options.D: After the balance is confirmed by staff

questions.explanation: The email specifies the form must be completed by March 10.

items.text: Just tried the new iced matcha latte at Campus Grounds! It's only $4.50 and so refreshing for studying. Perfect for this warm weather. They have it all week until 8 PM. You've got to try it!

questions.stem: What specific detail is given about the new drink?

options.A: Its price is four dollars and fifty cents.
options.B: It is a hot beverage for cold days.
options.C: It is available exclusively on weekends.

questions.explanation: The post states the iced matcha latte is 'only $4.50'.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the person who wrote the post?

options.A: They work at the Campus Grounds café.
options.B: They are dissatisfied with the drink's quality.
options.C: They enjoyed the drink they purchased.
options.D: They prefer to study in the library.

questions.explanation: The post uses positive language like 'so refreshing' and 'You've got to try it!', implying the writer enjoyed it.

items.text: Hi Alex, This confirms your tutoring appointment with Dr. Park for Thursday at 3:00 PM. Please meet in Room 204 of the Science Building. Remember to bring your textbook and notes. There's a $10 fee for cancellations with less than 24 hours' notice. Best, Tutoring Center

questions.stem: Where should Alex go for the confirmed appointment?

options.A: To the main library study room
options.B: To the front desk of the Tutoring Center
options.C: To Dr. Park's office in Anderson Hall
options.D: To Room 204 of the Science Building

questions.explanation: The email instructs Alex to meet in Room 204 of the Science Building.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the cancellation policy?

options.A: All cancellations require a doctor's note.
options.B: A fee is charged for last-minute cancellations.
options.C: Appointments can be rescheduled for free anytime.
options.D: The fee is refundable if you rebook later.

questions.explanation: The email mentions a $10 fee for cancellations with less than 24 hours' notice, implying a charge for last-minute changes.

items.text: Notice: HVAC maintenance in Dalton Hall is scheduled for April 5-7. During this period, room temperatures may fluctuate. Occupants are advised to dress in layers. For urgent issues, contact the Facilities Office at ext. 5555. We apologize for the inconvenience.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should occupants do during the maintenance period?

options.A: To vacate the building entirely for three days
options.B: To report all temperature changes immediately
options.C: To schedule their own repair appointments
options.D: To wear adaptable clothing like layers

questions.explanation: The notice advises occupants to dress in layers due to potential temperature fluctuations.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the nature of the upcoming work?

options.A: It will completely replace the building's heating system.
options.B: It might cause temporary discomfort for building users.
options.C: It is expected to lower everyone's utility bills.
options.D: It requires all classes in Dalton Hall to be canceled.

questions.explanation: The notice warns of temperature fluctuations and apologizes for inconvenience, implying possible temporary discomfort.

items.text: Spring Campus Cleanup! Join us this Saturday, April 20, from 10 AM to 1 PM. Meet at the Student Union. A $5 deposit is required for gloves and supplies, refundable upon return. Help keep our campus beautiful!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is the purpose of the $5?

options.B: To cover the cost of lunch for volunteers
options.C: To pay for parking near the Student Union
options.D: To secure equipment that will be returned

questions.explanation: The flyer states the deposit is for gloves and supplies and is refundable upon their return.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the cleanup event?

options.A: It is intended primarily for new students.
options.B: Volunteers must provide their own tools.
options.C: The organizers expect a very large turnout.
options.D: Participants are not paying a permanent fee.

questions.explanation: Since the $5 is a refundable deposit, participants will get their money back, meaning it is not a permanent cost.

items.text: Give Blood, Save Lives! Campus Blood Drive: Tuesday, October 30, 9 AM–4 PM. Location: Anderson Hall Gym. All donors receive a free T-shirt and snacks. Appointments recommended; walk-ins welcome. Bring photo ID.

questions.stem: Where will the blood drive take place?

options.B: At the University Hospital
options.C: Near the main library entrance

questions.explanation: The flyer clearly states the location is the Anderson Hall Gym.

questions.stem: The word 'walk-ins' in the flyer is closest in meaning to

options.B: volunteers assisting donors
options.C: donors without appointments

questions.explanation: In this context, 'walk-ins' refers to potential donors who arrive without a prior scheduled appointment.

items.text: Hi Jordan, Thank you for participating in our sleep study last Friday. Your $15 compensation is ready for pickup at the Psychology Department office, Room 312, any weekday from 9 AM to 5 PM. Bring your student ID. Best, Ms. Chen

questions.stem: What does Jordan need to bring to collect the compensation?

options.B: A printed copy of this email
options.C: A valid student identification card
options.D: A receipt from the sleep study

questions.explanation: The email instructs Jordan to 'Bring your student ID.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the pickup?

options.A: It must be scheduled in advance.
options.B: It is available during standard office hours.
options.C: The money will be sent via direct deposit.
options.D: Only the researcher can distribute it.

questions.explanation: The email states pickup is available 'any weekday from 9 AM to 5 PM,' which are typical business hours.

items.text: Dear Students, My regular office hours are canceled this Wednesday, November 8. I will hold extended hours instead on Thursday, November 9, from 1:00 to 5:00 PM in my usual office, Humanities 407. Please plan accordingly. Sincerely, Prof. Miller

questions.stem: When are Professor Miller's extended office hours?

options.B: On Wednesday morning
options.C: On Friday from 1 to 5 PM

questions.explanation: The email specifies extended hours on Thursday, November 9, from 1:00 to 5:00 PM.

questions.stem: The word 'accordingly' in the email is closest in meaning to

options.B: separately
options.C: appropriately

questions.explanation: Here, 'accordingly' means students should adjust their plans in an appropriate way based on this new schedule.

items.text: Notice: Unclaimed items from the Lost and Found in the University Center will be disposed of after Friday, May 3. Items may be claimed at the Information Desk until 6:00 PM on that date. Please present identification for verification.

questions.stem: According to the notice, by when must items be claimed?

options.A: Before the end of the day on May 2
options.B: By 6 PM on May 3
options.C: During the first week of May

questions.explanation: The notice states items may be claimed 'until 6:00 PM on that date,' with 'that date' being Friday, May 3.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about claiming a lost item?

options.A: A small fee is required for retrieval.
options.B: Only electronic items are held until May 3.
options.C: The Information Desk is open 24 hours.
options.D: The owner must prove their identity.

questions.explanation: The notice instructs claimants to 'present identification for verification,' implying proof of identity is needed.

items.text: Heads up! The Computer Lab in the library is offering FREE printing until this Friday! It's a great chance to print those final papers without spending $3.50 at the usual spot. Just bring your own paper. #CampusPerk

questions.stem: What is the usual cost for printing mentioned in the post?

options.B: $5.00 per document
options.C: A $10.00 semester fee

questions.explanation: The post states the free printing saves you from 'spending $3.50 at the usual spot.'

questions.stem: The phrase 'Heads up' in the post is closest in meaning to

options.B: An advance warning
options.C: Pay attention now

questions.explanation: In informal communication, 'Heads up' is used to give an early alert or warning about something.

items.text: Shuttle Detour Notice: Due to construction, the Green Route shuttle will be detoured from Monday, September 11, through Friday, September 15. During this period, temporary stops will be located at the North Parking Garage and outside Wilson Hall.

questions.stem: Why is the shuttle route being changed?

options.A: Because of a special campus event
options.B: Because of ongoing construction work
options.C: Due to driver shortages this week

questions.explanation: The notice begins, 'Due to construction,' which is the reason for the detour.

questions.stem: Where is one temporary stop during the detour?

options.B: Next to the Science Library
options.C: Outside Wilson Hall

questions.explanation: The notice lists 'outside Wilson Hall' as one of the temporary stops.

items.text: Vending machine refunds are limited to $2.00 per transaction. Submit a claim form to the Facilities Office in Anderson Hall by 5:00 PM on Friday, May 15. Include your student ID and a description of the issue.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the maximum refund amount for a vending machine issue?

options.B: $3.00 per transaction
options.C: $2.00 per transaction

questions.explanation: The notice states refunds are limited to $2.00 per transaction.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the refund process?

options.B: It involves submitting documentation.
options.C: It is available at any campus office.

questions.explanation: The requirement to submit a form with a description implies documentation is needed.

items.text: Hi Residents, A package for you is at the central mailroom. Please pick it up by 6 PM this Friday, March 7. You'll need your campus ID. Contact me with questions. Best, RA Jordan

questions.stem: According to the email, where should the package be collected?

options.B: In the resident advisor's office
options.C: At the central mailroom

questions.explanation: The email states the package is at the central mailroom.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the pickup deadline?

options.A: It is flexible if you contact the RA.
options.B: It is only for large packages.
options.C: It applies to weekend hours.
options.D: It is the last day for collection.

questions.explanation: The instruction to pick it up 'by' Friday implies it is the final day.

items.text: Dear Biology 101 Students, This is a reminder: Mandatory lab safety training is on Wednesday at 2:00 PM in Room 204. Attendance will be recorded. You can't participate in labs without it. Sincerely, Dr. Park

questions.stem: According to the email, when is the lab safety training?

options.B: On Tuesday at 3:00 PM
options.C: On Wednesday at 2:00 PM

questions.explanation: The email states the training is on Wednesday at 2:00 PM.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the training?

options.A: It is optional for new students.
options.B: It replaces the first lab session.
options.C: It is a requirement for lab work.
options.D: It will be offered multiple times.

questions.explanation: The email states students cannot participate in labs without it, implying it is a requirement.

items.text: Unclaimed items from the Lost & Found will be donated on Friday, May 10. Claim items at the Student Services desk in Union Building by 4:00 PM. A $5.00 retrieval fee applies to electronics and wallets.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the deadline to claim lost items?

options.B: Friday, May 10, by 4:00 PM
options.C: The following Monday

questions.explanation: The notice states items must be claimed by 4:00 PM on Friday, May 10.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about retrieving a lost wallet?

options.B: It requires an appointment.
options.C: It can be done after May 10.

questions.explanation: The notice states a $5.00 fee applies to wallets, implying a cost.

items.text: The elevator in Dalton Hall will be out of service from March 3-7 for maintenance. Please use the stairs or the elevator in the adjacent Science Building. We apologize for the inconvenience.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how long will the elevator be unavailable?

options.B: From March 3 to March 7
options.C: Throughout the month

questions.explanation: The notice states the elevator will be out of service from March 3-7.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Science Building elevator?

options.A: It is also scheduled for maintenance.
options.B: It is available as an alternative.
options.C: It is for faculty use only.
options.D: It is located far from Dalton Hall.

questions.explanation: The notice suggests using the elevator in the adjacent Science Building, implying it is an available alternative.

items.text: Dear Student, You have a package at the Campus Mail Center. Please collect it by Thursday. The center is open 9 AM-5 PM. Bring your student ID for verification. Regards, Mail Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what must be brought to collect the package?

options.B: The package notification email
options.C: A receipt for postage

questions.explanation: The email states to bring your student ID for verification.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the mail center's hours?

options.B: They include evening availability.
options.C: They change on weekends.
options.D: They are only for package pickup.

questions.explanation: The hours of 9 AM-5 PM are typical standard business hours.

items.text: South Lot B will be closed for repaving from April 10-12. Permit holders may park in North Lot A at no extra charge. Vehicles remaining in South Lot B after 7:00 AM on April 10 will be towed at the owner's expense.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where can permit holders park during the closure?

options.B: In any campus lot
options.C: In visitor parking

questions.explanation: The notice states permit holders may park in North Lot A.

options.A: It does not require an additional fee.
options.B: It is only for one day.
options.C: It requires a special permit.

questions.explanation: The notice states parking is 'at no extra charge,' implying no additional fee.

options.B: In the parking garage
options.C: At the main entrance
options.D: On the west side of the building

items.text: Hi Class, My office hours this week (Oct 15-19) are changed to 10:00 AM-12:00 PM, Tuesday and Thursday only, in my office, Room 312. I won't be available on Wednesday. Email for appointments. Prof. Chen

items.text: Bike racks on the Walker Hall sidewalk will be removed for repair from June 5-7. A temporary rack is available outside the gym. Bikes left on the Walker Hall racks after 8:00 AM on June 5 will be impounded; a $10.00 release fee applies.

options.D: Later in the afternoon on the same day

questions.explanation: The notice states service will be restored by 6:30 AM.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Wi-Fi outage?

options.A: It will only impact student residence halls.
options.B: It is planned for a time of low network usage.
options.C: It will cause a delay in all morning classes.
options.D: It is due to a sudden and unexpected failure.

questions.explanation: Since the maintenance is scheduled for very early morning (2:00 AM to 6:00 AM), it can be inferred this is a time of low usage.

items.text: Dear Student, Your meal plan has a remaining balance of $45.75. To receive a refund, complete the online form by May 1. Refunds under $50 will be issued as a credit to your student account. Sincerely, Dining Services

questions.stem: By what date must the student act to obtain a refund?

options.A: By the final day of the academic year
options.B: By the first day of the month of May
options.C: Within one week of receiving this email
options.D: Before the end of the current semester

questions.explanation: The email states the form must be completed by May 1.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the refund for this student's balance?

options.A: It will be sent as a paper check by mail.
options.B: The student must visit the office in person to claim it.
options.C: The money will be applied to their university account.
options.D: It is only available for balances over fifty dollars.

questions.explanation: Since the balance is $45.75 (under $50) and the email says refunds under $50 are issued as a credit to the student account, it can be inferred the money will be applied there.

items.text: For vending machine refunds, contact the Business Office in Room 110. The maximum refund is $2.00 per incident. Provide the machine location and the item code. Refunds are processed weekdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM.

questions.stem: What is the highest amount one can receive back for a single vending machine issue?

options.B: Five dollars
options.C: The full price of the purchased item
options.D: A replacement item of equal value

questions.explanation: The notice states the maximum refund is $2.00 per incident.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about obtaining a refund?

options.A: One cannot get a refund on weekends.
options.B: A receipt from the machine is not necessary.
options.C: Refunds are handled by the Facilities Office.
options.D: The process can be completed entirely online.

questions.explanation: Since refunds are processed weekdays from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, it can be inferred they are not available on weekends.

items.text: Campus Photo Contest! Submit your best shot by May 15. $5 entry fee. Win a $100 gift card! Digital entries only to photoclub@university.edu. Include your name and student ID. Get creative and show your school spirit!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is required for contest entry?

options.A: A physical print of the photograph
options.B: A payment of five dollars
options.C: A recommendation from a professor

questions.explanation: The flyer states there is a '$5 entry fee,' which is a required payment.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the contest's theme?

options.B: It is focused on portrait photography.
options.C: It requires images of campus buildings.
options.D: It is only for advanced photographers.

questions.explanation: The flyer encourages participants to 'show your school spirit,' implying the theme relates to university pride.

items.text: Dear Biology Club Members, Our meeting this Friday is rescheduled to 4:00 PM. It will now be in Room 204 of Science Hall. Dr. Park will still present. Please update your calendars. We'll see you there. Best, Club President Alex

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the new meeting time?

options.A: At four o'clock in the afternoon
options.B: At two o'clock in the afternoon
options.C: At nine o'clock in the morning
options.D: At seven o'clock in the evening

questions.explanation: The email specifies the meeting is 'rescheduled to 4:00 PM,' which is four o'clock in the afternoon.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the original meeting plan?

options.B: It was scheduled for a different time or place.
options.C: It featured a different speaker.
options.D: It required members to bring materials.

questions.explanation: Since the meeting is 'rescheduled' with a new time and room, the original plan must have been different.

items.text: Found the perfect quiet study spot! The basement of the main library, near the archives, is super peaceful. Hardly anyone goes there. Great for focusing before finals. Open until 10 PM on weekdays. Check it out!

questions.stem: According to the post, where is the recommended study area?

options.B: In the campus coffee shop
options.C: In the library basement

questions.explanation: The post explicitly recommends 'The basement of the main library.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the spot's popularity?

options.B: It is not widely used.
options.C: It requires a reservation.
options.D: It is mainly for group work.

questions.explanation: The post states 'Hardly anyone goes there,' implying it is not widely used or popular.

items.text: Hi Maria, This confirms your math tutoring appointment with Mr. Lee for Thursday at 3:00 PM. Please meet in the Learning Center, Room 12. Don't forget your textbook and calculator. The first session is free, but later ones are $15 per hour. See you then!

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the cost of the confirmed session?

options.B: A ten-dollar deposit
options.C: Five dollars for materials

questions.explanation: The email states 'The first session is free,' meaning there is no charge for this confirmed appointment.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about future tutoring sessions?

options.A: They will be held in a different building.
options.B: They will focus on a different subject.
options.C: They will be at a different time of day.

questions.explanation: The email notes that 'later ones are $15 per hour,' implying future sessions are not free and require payment.

items.text: Free yoga on the main quad this Saturday at 10 AM! It's a great way to de-stress. Bring your own mat and water. The class is for all levels. Sponsored by the Wellness Center. Hope to see you there!

questions.stem: According to the post, when is the yoga class?

options.B: On Friday afternoon
options.C: On Sunday evening

questions.explanation: The post specifies the class is 'this Saturday at 10 AM,' which is Saturday morning.

questions.stem: What should participants bring to the class?

options.B: A student ID card
options.C: A personal yoga mat

questions.explanation: The instruction 'Bring your own mat' indicates participants need a personal yoga mat.
